title: chrysanthemum indicum subsp. albescens
intro: Chrysanthemum indicum subsp. albescens, also known as the white-flowered Chinese chrysanthemum, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Chrysanthemum indicum subsp. albescens include reducing fever, relieving headaches and sore throats, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
